subject: [PATCH v2 0/4] mm: improve large folio readahead and alignment for exec memory

reviews.author: WANG Rui
reviews.summary: Reviewer WANG Rui noted that the patch may overlook another path creating huge page mappings for read-only code segments, specifically through khugepaged even when filesystems don't support large folios, and suggested using a different approach to optimize khugepaged-based mappings.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah, it looks like this might be overlooking another path that can create
huge page mappings for read-only code segments: even when the filesystem
(e.g. btrfs without experimental) didn't support large folios,
READ_ONLY_THP_FOR_FS still allowed read-only file-backed code segments
to be collapsed into huge page mappings via khugepaged.

As Wilcox pointed out, it may take quite some time for many filesystems
to gain full large folio support? So what I'm trying to clarify is that
using mapping_max_folio_size() on this path is not favorable for
khugepaged-based optimizations.

Thanks,
Rui

2026-03-29.patch_summary: This patch series improves large folio readahead and alignment for executable memory by introducing a generic, architecture-independent solution to replace the arch-specific exec_folio_order() function. The patches address two main issues: the mmap_miss heuristic that disables readahead after 100 page faults, and the suboptimal static order returned by exec_folio_order() for non-4K page sizes. The new approach uses preferred_exec_order() to sync mmap_readahead with zone high watermarks, and extends max alignment to mapping_max_folio_size().

2026-03-30.patch_summary: This patch series improves large folio readahead and alignment for executable memory by introducing a generic, architecture-independent solution to replace the arch-specific exec_folio_order() function. The patches address two main issues: the mmap_miss heuristic that disables readahead after 100 page faults, and the suboptimal static order returned by exec_folio_order() for non-4K page sizes. The new approach uses preferred_exec_order() to sync mmap_readahead with zone high watermarks, and extends max alignment to mapping_max_folio_size().
